text.title: GitHub Actions — Security Hardening Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Compromised tag points to malicious commit; secrets exfil.
antipattern.fix: Pin every `uses:` to a SHA via dependabot or manual rotation.

antipattern.symptom: AWS key leaks in fork log; full account breach.
antipattern.fix: Switch to OIDC with subject-scoped trust policy.

antipattern.symptom: Compromised step writes a tag; release rolled back.
antipattern.fix: Add `permissions: read-all` workflow-wide and scope per job.

antipattern.symptom: Fork PR executes arbitrary code with secrets.
antipattern.fix: Remove the pattern or switch to pull_request without secret access.
